title: Customer Churn Prediction
summary: Predicting which telecom customers are likely to churn using classification models, to support targeted retention campaigns.
problem: A telecom dataset with ~7,000 customers needed a model to flag likely churners so retention offers could be targeted instead of blanket-applied.
approach: Cleaned and explored the dataset, engineered tenure and usage-based features, then compared logistic regression, random forest, and gradient boosting models using cross-validation.
results: Gradient boosting achieved 84% recall on churners with 79% overall accuracy, identified contract type and monthly charges as the strongest churn predictors.
